full_text: I just vi coded this app, which turns the images of a physical Japanese book into language learning materials, so here I can capture page and take a photo of the page I'm reading.And then the AI is going to extract the text from the image This is done by Geminizes image processing capability Now the AI has turned a text on my physical book which is vertical into very neat horizontal text which I can then manipulate for example if I don't understand this term I can just select and then tap explain selection and its going to explain the term clearly in context in the format that I like which also includes an example sentence I can save this to my notebook this applies to not just vocabulary.but also phrases because the model can basically explain anything in this specific context, whether it's vocabulary phrases, grammar or even entire sentence I also have a deep read mode while the deep read mode is loading it's going to display the entries in my notebook in carousel so that I can review my vocabulary while waiting in deep read mode it parss the text sentence by sentence for each sentence is going to first explain the structure in a grammar and then extract a key vocabulary and.Expressions with in context explanation, which I can also add to my vocabulary notebook with one click and if I want, I can also show translation, but by default it's not shown so that I need to read the Japanese version myself and make sense of it first This is a great way to turn the text into material that you want to actually spend time reading deeply and learning from And this is your notebook Basically everything you've saved in the app is here. you can also turn this into flashcardma where the front is.The word and the back is the explanation.All your previous parse text is saved in the app so that when you go back to deep Read is all cached and you don't need to load it again, this is a great way to turn static physical books into interactive learning materials for languages and also makes it easier to learn on the go.
